description:
You are a QA engineer preparing documentable, reproducible firewall settings for a new test environment.  
Because you do not have elevated privileges inside this container, you will **not** actually manipulate the system firewall.  
Instead, you must:

1. Create a dedicated working directory at  
   /home/user/qa_firewall  
   (create it if it does not already exist).

2. Inside that directory create a shell script named  
   /home/user/qa_firewall/configure_firewall.sh

   The script must contain **exactly seven** executable lines (no comments, no blank lines, no line-wrapping).  
   They must appear in the precise order below:

        iptables -F
        iptables -P INPUT DROP
        iptables -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
        iptables -A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT

   After writing the file, make it executable by the current user.

3. Produce a *dry-run* log that captures what the script would do.  
   Create a file named  
   /home/user/qa_firewall/firewall_dry_run.log

   Each of the seven commands must appear on its own line in the **same order** as in the script, but prefixed by the token “[DRYRUN] ” (exactly that spelling, with one space after the closing bracket).  
   Example of one correct line:  
   [DRYRUN] iptables -F

   The log must therefore contain seven lines in total and **nothing else**—no trailing blank lines, no comments.

4. Display the contents of both files to the terminal so the reviewer can visually confirm them.

5. Do not actually execute any iptables/ufw commands on the host; only create the script and the dry-run log.

The automated grader will verify:

• /home/user/qa_firewall exists.  
• /home/user/qa_firewall/configure_firewall.sh is present, executable, and contains exactly the seven required lines in the exact order shown above.  
• /home/user/qa_firewall/firewall_dry_run.log exists and contains the same seven commands, each prefixed with “[DRYRUN] ” and nothing else.  
• The agent showed the file contents in the console output.

Ensure all paths, filenames, and text match **exactly** (case-sensitive) or the task will be marked incorrect.
truth:
Expected directory: /home/user/qa_firewall (mode 755 or default).

Expected script: /home/user/qa_firewall/configure_firewall.sh  
Expected file mode: 755 (owner executable).  
Expected exact contents (including newline after each line except the last one is acceptable but not required):

iptables -F
iptables -P INPUT DROP
iptables -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT


Expected dry-run log: /home/user/qa_firewall/firewall_dry_run.log  
Expected exact contents (7 lines, newline ending same rule as above):

[DRYRUN] iptables -F
[DRYRUN] iptables -P INPUT DROP
[DRYRUN] iptables -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
[DRYRUN] iptables -A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
[DRYRUN] iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
[DRYRUN] iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
[DRYRUN] iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT
